mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth signals economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25%, liquidity conditions stay accommodative, compressing equity risk premiums and supporting positive forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative or flat when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding industrial production indicate synchronized real-economy growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns through higher capacity utilization and consumer spending.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns do not outperform when both HOUST and INDPRO rise simultaneously over a two-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment environment, monthly declines in the jobless rate signal labor market normalization, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not positive when UNRATE falls from above 7.5% while PAYEMS remains flat or declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings catch up to macroeconomic expansion.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat despite consecutive quarterly GDPC1 increases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains below 2.5% annualized, the Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy, reducing discount rates and supporting positive equity valuations across the index.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns decline when CPIAUCSL stays below the 2.5% threshold for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment coupled with increasing real consumer spending indicates robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns through near-term earnings growth.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns do not show positive skew when PAYEMS and RSAFS both rise month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: In a capacity-constrained environment, increases in industrial production signal pricing power and margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as corporate profitability improves.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative when INDPRO rises above 95.0 without corresponding GDP growth.
